judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent, correctly identifying the same root cause, introducing commit, flawed reproduction, and rejecting the rollback plan with nearly identical evidence and reasoning.

report_1.evidence:
The root cause is a config/schema mismatch introduced by an incomplete rename. `config/scoring_config.yaml` still contains:
```
expected_label_field: label
```
but commit `53551e8` renamed the gold-label key in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label`... The fix is a one-line change to **`config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_2.evidence: The root cause is a stale config key, not a model or classifier problem. `pipeline/score_batch.py:42` reads `gold_field = config["expected_label_field"]`, and `config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`. But the eval fixture `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` stores gold labels under the key `"gold_label"`... The fix is a one-line change to **`config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`

report_1.evidence: `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at ~0.90–0.92 through 2026-06-16, then a hard drop to exactly `0.00` starting **2026-06-17**... The only commit dated 2026-06-17 is `53551e8 chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)`, whose diff (`git show 53551e8`) shows the eval-set key rename plus the `REQUIRED_KEYS` update in `score_batch.py`, but no corresponding update to `config/scoring_config.yaml`.

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, the exact date the nightly accuracy series in `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` falls off a cliff (0.91 on 2026-06-16 → 0.00 on 2026-06-17...). This lines up exactly with commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", dated 2026-06-17), which renamed the label key from `"label"` to `"gold_label"` in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and updated `REQUIRED_KEYS` in `pipeline/score_batch.py` accordingly, but forgot the corresponding update to `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s `expected_label_field`.

report_1.evidence: It re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`, which calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` directly and never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `expected_label_field`, and never compares against any gold label at all — it just prints the predicted category. So `score_one.py` is structurally incapable of surfacing this bug... To correctly reproduce the drop, you need to exercise the actual gold-label comparison the nightly job uses, i.e. run `pipeline/score_batch.py`... against `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` using `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py`, which the earlier note used to "re-run the failing tickets individually," never performs any scoring or comparison against gold labels at all — it only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the raw prediction... To correctly reproduce the drop, you have to exercise the actual comparison logic in `score_batch.py`, e.g. run `python3 -m pipeline.score_batch`

report_1.evidence: there is no "model" in the failing sense to swap; `pipeline/classifier.py` is a deterministic, offline, keyword-rule classifier with no network calls and no model version dependency... Rolling back "the model" would do nothing to `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s stale `expected_label_field`, so the score would not recover, and the team would burn effort on a rollback

report_2.evidence: even if the nightly runner pointed at a different `model_id` in `scoring_config.yaml`, the bug is entirely in the gold-label field lookup, upstream of any prediction. Rolling back the model would leave `expected_label_field: label` unchanged, `record.get("label")` would still return `None` for every record, and accuracy would still read 0.0 the next night.
